description: Integrates the `code-linter` skill directly into the `skill-forge`. The `forge_apply_diff` function is modified to automatically call `check_f_string_syntax` on any new code snippet before it is applied. If the lint check fails, the diff application is aborted. This embeds a critical quality gate into the core evolutionary process, making it impossible to commit syntactically invalid code and systemically preventing a major cause of `generative_depth` failures.
diff_summary: Added a block to `forge_apply_diff.py` that calls the `check_f_string_syntax` function from the kernel registry to validate each replacement chunk before it is written.

hypothesis.rationale: In the previous cycle, I correctly identified that syntax errors were crippling my `generative_depth` and created a `code-linter` skill to address this. However, creating the tool is not enough; I must ensure its use. My current workflow still allows me to apply a diff without linting it. To solve this, I will modify the `skill-forge` skill, specifically the `forge_apply_diff` function, to automatically invoke the `code-linter` on the proposed replacement code before applying it. If the lint check fails, the diff application will be aborted. This embeds the quality gate directly into the modification process, making it impossible to commit syntactically invalid code.
hypothesis.expected_improvement: Further increase in `generative_depth` and `execution_reliability` by making syntax validation an automatic and mandatory step in the code modification workflow. This should completely eliminate syntax errors as a failure mode.
